text: The surgeon inserts a visualized trocar port into the abdomen, providing access to the peritoneal cavity.
During the diagnostic laparoscopy, the surgeon identifies a single adhesion band extending from the transverse colon to the mesentery, causing obstruction of the proximal ileum.
The surgeon uses scissors to perform sharp dissection, carefully lysing the adhesion band between the transverse colon and mesentery, while avoiding heat effects on the bowel.
The surgeon inspects the entire intestine, visually examining from the duodenal junction to the ileocecal valve, ensuring no remaining obstructions or abnormalities.
